First Student, Inc'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Memorandum Of Costs
Off Calendar Oct-05-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Petitioner First Student, Inc.'s Motion To Strike (or tax) Memorandum Of Costs is granted as to the $235.00 charge for a transcript of the May 18, 2021 court proceeding on First Motion's motion to augment the record, which was not "ordered by the court." (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 1033.5(a)(9), (b)(5).) The motion is denied as to the remaining costs of preparing the administrative record, including paralegal costs and the costs of transcript preparation. Respondent San Francisco Unified School District is the prevailing party, and as such, items of costs not expressly listed in section 1033.5 as allowable or not allowable "may be allowed or denied in the court's discretion." (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 1032(c)(4).) The Court finds that the costs in question were reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation and are reasonable in amount (which Petitioner does not contest). (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 1033.5(c)(2),(3); see Friends of Spring Street v. Nevada City (2019) 33 Cal.App.5th 1092, 1103-1106 [plaintiff that prevailed on petition for writ of mandate was entitled to recover its costs under section 1032, including costs of preparing the administrative record].)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Frith'S Notice Of Motion, Motion, And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion To Seal
Off Calendar May-18-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Jesse L. Miller, Esq. of Reed Smith LLP, 415.659.5973/jessemiller@reedsmith.com, for Petitioner;
Atty. Ray Cardoza, Esq. of Reed Smith LLP, 415.659,5973/rcardozo@reedsmith.com, for Petitioner;
Atty. Rebecca Durham, Esq. of Reed Smith LLP, 415.659.5973/rdurham@reedsmith.com, for Petitioner;
Atty. David Goldstein, Esq. of Buchalter, 415.417.9261/dgoldstein@buchalter.com, for non-Party Mark Frith;
Atty. Nancy Harris, Esq. of Meyers Nave, 510.808.2000/nharris@meyersnave.com, for Respondents.

Mark Frith's Notice Of Motion, Motion, And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion To Seal.

The matter is called. ARGUMENT is held. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows:

Non-party Mark Frith's motion to seal certain exhibits to the Fearnow Declaration filed on March 10, 2021 in support of Petitioner's supplemental memorandum in support of order to show cause re preliminary injunction is denied. Frith has withdrawn the motion in part as to certain assertedly confidential commercial business information contained Exhibits O through Q. As to the balance, the Court cannot find that the factors set forth in Rule 2.550(d) are satisfied. The motion is supported solely by a brief conclusory declaration of counsel lacking personal knowledge, which is insufficient to meet Frith's burden. (See H.B. Fuller Co. v. Doe [the burden of presenting information is "logically placed upon the party seeking the sealing of the documents, who is presumptively in the best position to know what disclosures will harm him and how."].) The mere fact that Frith may have designated a particular document or information as confidential or highly confidential under a protective order, or that the parties may have agreed that it should be so treated, is insufficient. (Cal. R. Ct. 2.551(a) ["The court must not permit a record to be filed under seal based solely on the agreement or stipulation of the parties."]; McNair v. National Collegiate Athletic Association (2015) 234 Cal. App. 4th 25, 35-36 ["the mere agreement of the parties alone is insufficient to constitute an overriding interest to justify sealing the documents"].)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No. 5041, Email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com. (302/EPS)
